activities.description: Activity represents a change or action in the activity feed of a Task, Deal, Project, Company, or Person. Activities track events like creation, updates, deletions, comments, and other changes made to items in the system.
activities.descriptionShort: changes or events in the activity feed of tasks, deals, projects, and other entities

event.description: The type of change (create, update, delete, etc.)

changeset.description: The object containing the changes made to the item

itemId.description: ID of the item this activity is about

itemName.description: Name or title of the item

itemType.description: Type of item (Comment, Booking, Membership etc.)

itemDeletedAt.description: Timestamp of when the item was deleted

parentDeletedAt.description: Timestamp of when the parent item was deleted

rootDeletedAt.description: Timestamp of when the root item was deleted

dealId.description: ID of the deal this activity is related to

dealIsBudget.description: Indicates whether the deal is a budget

taskId.description: ID of the task this activity is related to

pageId.description: ID of the page this activity is related to

companyId.description: ID of the company this activity is related to

bookingId.description: ID of the booking this activity is related to

personId.description: ID of the person this activity is related to

madeByAutomation.description: Whether the activity was triggered by an automation

creator.description: The person who created/triggered the activity

comment.description: The comment associated with this activity

email.description: The email associated with this activity

attachment.description: The attachment associated with this activity

before.description: Filter activities created before this date

after.description: Filter activities created after this date

agent_configs.description: Agent Config stores the customizable settings for an AI agent. Each config belongs to one agent and defines the system instructions (prompt) and the set of tools the agent can use. Updating the config changes the agent behavior without recreating the agent.

agents.description: Agent is an AI-powered assistant that can perform tasks and actions within Productive. Agents have a custom role that defines what the agent can do (its permissions), an optional manager, and a configuration that defines their instructions and tools. Memberships control who can see and use the agent.
agents.descriptionShort: AI assistants that perform tasks and actions

allocations.description:
Allocation is a read-only view of planned time for people in the Resource Planner.
Unlike bookings (which can be created/updated/deleted), allocations are aggregated read-only records
used for reporting and visibility into how people's time is scheduled.
Service allocations represent time scheduled on a project service (billable work).
Event allocations represent time off (vacation, sick leave, remote work, etc).
Allocations are stored in the bookings table but exclude canceled, rejected, and deleted bookings.
allocations.descriptionShort: read-only views of scheduled time for people — project work and time-off allocations

hours.description: Hours per day. Used with booking method "per day".

time.description: Time per day in minutes. Used with booking method "1" (per day).

totalTime.description: Total time in minutes across the entire allocation period. Used with booking method "3" (total hours).

totalWorkingDays.description: Total number of working days in the allocation period

percentage.description: Percentage of the workday allocated (0-100). Used with booking method "2" (percentage).

bookingMethodId.description: Method used to specify the time allocation: "1" = per day, "2" = percentage, "3" = total hours

bookingType.description: Type of allocation: "service" for project work, "event" for time off/absences

after.description: Filter allocations that end on or after this date (ended_on >= value)

before.description: Filter allocations that start on or before this date (started_on <= value)

service.description: Service (project work) this person is allocated to

event.description: Absence category this person is allocated to. Present when booking_type is "event".

client.description: Client company associated with the service allocation

responsible.description: Project manager responsible for the service allocation

allocations.queryHints:
“After” and “before” date filters can be used to scope allocations to a specific time range.
The “booking_type” filter can be applied to distinguish service allocations (work) from event allocations (time off).

approval_policies.description:
Approval Policy defines reusable rules for who must approve submissions before they are recognized and billable.
Policies are typed by what they govern:
- Event (type_id=1): absence/time-off requests — approvers review bookings for vacation, sick leave, remote work.
- Budget (type_id=2): time entries and expenses on budgets — controls whether tracked time and logged expenses require review before affecting revenue and profitability.
- Deal (type_id=3): time entries and expenses on deals — same as budget policies but for sales deals (Beta).

Each policy specifies approver roles (manager, budget/deal owner, project manager, or specific people) and approval mode:
- No approval needed: submissions are auto-approved.
- Any one of the listed approvers: single approval is sufficient.
- All approvers: every listed approver must approve; a single rejection declines the request.

Policies can be set as default (auto-assigned to new budgets/deals) or assigned in bulk.
A policy only takes effect if the corresponding organization-wide approval feature is enabled (time approval, expense approval, or absence approval).
Policies can be archived when no longer needed.
approval_policies.descriptionShort: rules defining who approves absences, time entries, or expenses — one or all approvers

approval_policy_assignments.description:
Approval Policy Assignment links an approval policy to a target entity — a person, budget, or deal.
Each target can have at most one active assignment per policy type.

- target_type "person": governs absence/time-off approval for that person's leave requests.
- target_type "budget": governs time entry and expense approval on that budget.
- target_type "deal": governs time entry and expense approval on that deal.

Without an assignment, a policy has no effect on a specific entity.
approval_policy_assignments.descriptionShort: which approval policy governs absences, time entries, or expenses for a person, budget, or deal

approvalPolicy.description: The governing approval policy. Include to get policy details such as approvers, approval mode, and type.

person.description: The person this policy is assigned to. Only present when target_type is "person".

deal.description: The deal or budget this policy is assigned to. Only present when target_type is "budget" or "deal".

approval_policy_assignments.queryHints:
Filter by target_type and target_id together to find the assignment for a specific entity.
Always include approval_policy to get the full policy details alongside the assignment.

approval_statuses.description:
Approval Status is a per-approver record tracking one person's approval decision on a booking (absence), time entry, or expense.
When a submission triggers an approval workflow, one Approval Status is created for each designated approver in the policy.

Each record contains:
- The target entity (booking, time_entry, or expense) and its target_type: event (absence booking), time (time entry), or expense (expense).
- The designated approver (who should review) and the actual_approver (who performed the action — may differ if someone else acts on their behalf).
- The linked approval_workflow (the policy that generated this status).
- An optional note explaining the approval or rejection reason.

Approval statuses are system-managed — they are created automatically when submissions enter the approval flow and updated when approvers take action.
Only after all required approvers approve (based on the policy's mode) does the target entity become recognized, billable, and available for invoicing.
approval_statuses.descriptionShort: per-approver decisions on an absence, time entries, or expenses — auto-created by the approval policy

note.description: Note associated with the approval or rejection

targetType.description: Type of the target entity for approval

actualApprover.description: Person who actually performed the approval or rejection

approvalWorkflow.description: Approval workflow/policy used for this approval

booking.description: Associated booking if the approval target is a booking

expense.description: Associated expense if the approval target is an expense

timeEntry.description: Associated time entry if the approval target is a time entry

attachments.description: Attachment is a file uploaded to a Task, Deal, Page (Doc), Comment, or Expense. Attachments store documents, images, and other files related to work. The URL field provides a download link to access the file content.

url.description: File URL of the attachment. Use this URL to download and read the file.

automations.description:
Automation defines a rule that automatically triggers actions based on events or schedules.
Every rule has three parts: When (trigger), Check if (optional conditions), Then (actions).

Trigger types (type_id):
- Event (1): fires when a target object is created, updated, deleted, or commented on.
- Time (2): fires on a schedule (daily, weekly, monthly) at a specified time.

Target types (target_id): task (1), deal (2), budget (3), invoice (4), survey response (5), project (6), booking (7), expense (8), company (9), employee (10).

Available actions: send Slack message, send email, create task, create subtask, update field, add comment.
Conditions narrow when the rule fires (e.g., only if deal owner is a specific person, or task type matches a value).
Automations can be scoped to specific projects or applied across all projects (resolve_on_all_projects).
Each automation tracks monthly_runs and monthly_action_runs against the organization's action limit.
automations.descriptionShort: rules triggering actions (e.g. task creation) on events or schedules

query.description: Keyword or phrase for fuzzy text matching / filtering of automations

bill_items.description:
Bill Item is a line entry on a Bill, linking a specific expense (from the purchase order) to a received quantity.
Each bill item records how many units of a particular expense have been received in this delivery.
The sum of bill item quantities across all bills for an expense determines how much has been received vs ordered.
bill_items.descriptionShort: line items on a bill — links a PO expense to a received quantity

bill.description: Parent bill this item belongs to

expense.description: Expense this bill item is for

bills.description:
Bill records the receipt of goods or services ordered through a Purchase Order.
Each bill belongs to exactly one purchase order and contains Bill Items that specify the received quantity per expense.

As bills are recorded, the parent purchase order's payment status updates automatically:
not received → partially received → fully received.
Bills include the vendor's invoice number, date, due date, and description for reconciliation.
Currency is inherited from the purchase order.
bills.descriptionShort: receipt records against a purchase order — tracks what was received from a vendor

purchaseOrder.description: Parent purchase order this bill is for

bookings.description:
Booking represents planned time allocation in the Resource Planner — the core of resource scheduling in Productive.
A booking must have exactly one of service_id (work booking) or event_id (absence booking), never both.

Two booking types:
- Work bookings (booking_type=1): schedule a person on a Service in a Budget or Deal. Affect availability, contribute to scheduled revenue (for billable services), and appear as time tracking suggestions. Can optionally be linked to a Task for task-level planning.
- Absence bookings (booking_type=2): schedule time off or remote work on an Absence Category (Event). Time-off bookings reduce availability; remote work bookings do not. Deduct from the person's entitlement allocation. Can go through absence approval if enabled.

Time allocation methods:
- Hours per day (booking_method=1): fixed hours each day of the booking period.
- Percentage (booking_method=2): percentage of the person's daily capacity (e.g. 50% of an 8-hour day = 4 hours).
- Total hours (booking_method=3): total hours spread evenly across the date range.

Booking status:
- Confirmed (draft=false): firm allocation that counts toward capacity and scheduled revenue.
- Tentative (draft=true): soft allocation for potential work. Included in availability calculations but communicated as tentative. Can be toggled to confirmed later.

Bookings can be canceled (canceled_at set) rather than deleted, preserving history.
Automatic time tracking can be enabled per booking — confirmed bookings auto-generate time entries at midnight.
Bookings can be split, repeated (weekly/monthly), and duplicated. Multiple people can be booked on the same service simultaneously.
The overwrite action on absence bookings automatically splits overlapping work bookings to accommodate the absence period.
bookings.descriptionShort: planned work or absence — schedules a person on a service or absence category

overwrite.name: overwrite
overwrite.description: Overwrite an absence booking to resolve conflicts. Call this action on the ABSENCE booking ID (the booking with event). This will automatically split any overlapping work bookings to accommodate the absence period. NEVER call this on work booking IDs.

time.description: Total time booked in minutes. Required for booking methods "1" (time per day) and "3" (total time). Not used with booking method "2" (percentage).

percentage.description: Percentage of the day booked (0-100). Required for booking method "2" (percentage). Not used with booking methods "1" or "3".

canceledAt.description: Timestamp when the booking was canceled. Null for active bookings.

canceled.description: Filter by canceled status. Use false to get active (non-canceled) bookings, true to get canceled bookings.

after.description: Filter bookings that end on or after this date (ended_on >= value). Use to find bookings active within a date range.

before.description: Filter bookings that start on or before this date (started_on <= value). Use to find bookings active within a date range.

approvalStatus.description: Filter by approval status. Approved=1, Pending=2, Rejected=3, Canceled=5.

companyId.description: Filter bookings by client company (via service → deal → company)

budgetId.description: Filter bookings by deal/budget (via service → deal)

projectId.description: Filter bookings by project/workspace (via service → deal → project)

billingTypeId.description: Filter bookings by the billing type of their service.

service.description: Service for work bookings. Mutually exclusive with event (absence category) — a booking must have exactly one of service or event, never both, never neither.

event.description: Absence category for absence bookings (e.g. vacation, sick leave). Mutually exclusive with service — a booking must have exactly one of service or event, never both, never neither.

bookings.queryHints: Users typically care about active bookings, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for bookings should be: { is_canceled: {eq: false} }

calendar_events.description: Calendar Event represents an event from an external calendar integration (Google Calendar or Outlook). These are read-only events synced from a person's connected calendar, showing meetings, out-of-office time, and other scheduled events. Recurring events carry recurring_event_id that links all instances of the same series.
calendar_events.descriptionShort: events synced from an external calendar integration (google cal or outlook)

startDate.description: Start date of the event (YYYY-MM-DD format)

endDate.description: End date of the event (YYYY-MM-DD format)

startTime.description: Start time of the event in HH:MM:SS format (null for all-day events)

endTime.description: End time of the event in HH:MM:SS format (null for all-day events)

eventType.description: Type of calendar event. Google Calendar values: "default", "outOfOffice", "focusTime", "workingLocation". Outlook values: "free", "tentative", "busy", "oof", "workingElsewhere", "unknown".

recurringEventId.description: Identifier linking all instances of a recurring event series. Shared across all expanded instances of the same recurring meeting.

integrationIds.description: Filter calendar events by integration IDs. Use to scope events to specific calendar integrations.

calendarIds.description: Filter calendar events by calendar IDs. Use to scope events to specific calendars within an integration.

person.description: Array of person IDs to filter calendar events by. Returns events for any of the specified people. Use to scope events to specific individuals.

comments.description:
Comment is a message in the activity feed of a Task, Deal, Company, or Person.
Comments support rich text formatting and can include attachments.
Hidden comments are private notes visible only to the creator.

When creating a comment, provide exactly one parent relationship (task, deal, company, or person).
Mentions format: @[Person:ID:Name]
comments.descriptionShort: messages in the activity feed of a task, deal, company, or person

hidden.description: Hidden comments are only seen by the creator

project.description: Filter comments on tasks within a specific project

companies.description:
Company represents a client organization or the user's own organization.
Client companies are the central hub for managing client relationships — they link to deals, budgets, projects, contacts, and invoices.

Each company can define default invoicing settings (subsidiary, invoice template, tax rate, payment terms) that auto-apply when creating new deals, budgets, or invoices for that client.
Companies support hierarchical structures via parent_company_id, tags for categorization, and custom fields for additional data.
Rate cards can be linked to companies to define client-specific pricing.
The organization's own company is used for internal (non-billable) projects and as the default rate card source.
Companies can be archived when no longer active.
companies.descriptionShort: client organizations linked to deals, budgets, projects, and invoices — with invoicing settings and rate cards

defaultSubsidiaryId.description: Subsidiary used by default when creating new deals or budgets for this company

dueDays.description: Default payment terms in days for invoices

contact.description: Contact and address information (address, phone, email, etc.)

tagList.description: Tags assigned to the company for categorization

archivedAt.description: When the company was archived (null if active)

lastActivityAt.description: When the last activity occurred on the company

subscriberIds.description: IDs of people subscribed to this company

parentCompanyId.description: ID of the parent company in a hierarchical company structure

companies.queryHints: Users typically care about active companies, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for companies should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

contact_entries.description: Contact Entry stores contact information (email, phone, address, website) for people, companies, invoices, subsidiaries, and other contactable entities. Each entry has a type and belongs to one contactable entity via a polymorphic relationship.
contact_entries.descriptionShort: contact data (email, phone, address) for people or companies

contactableType.description: Type of entity this contact entry belongs to

type.description: Type of contact entry (address, email, phone, website, bill_from, bill_to)

address.description: Street address (when type is address, bill_from, or bill_to)

billingAddress.description: Whether this is a billing address

company.description: Company this contact entry belongs to

person.description: Person this contact entry belongs to

invoice.description: Invoice this contact entry belongs to

subsidiary.description: Subsidiary this contact entry belongs to

purchaseOrder.description: Purchase order this contact entry belongs to

contracts.description:
Contract defines a recurring (retainer) arrangement where a Budget automatically regenerates at set intervals.
Intervals include weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, semiannual, and annual. The next occurrence date determines when Productive creates a new budget.

Each contract is linked to a template deal/budget that serves as the blueprint for each new occurrence.
Configurable per-recurrence settings:
- Roll over unused hours: carry forward unused (or overused) hours from one period to the next for accurate retainer tracking.
- Copy expenses: duplicate expenses (with amounts, custom fields, descriptions) into each new budget. Payment status resets to unpaid.
- Copy purchase order number: maintain the same PO reference across all recurring budgets.
- Automatic invoice drafts can be configured to generate with each new occurrence.

Contracts have a start date, optional end date (for indefinite recurrence, leave blank), and track the next occurrence date.
The next occurrence can also be triggered manually for planning ahead.
contracts.descriptionShort: recurring retainers — auto-generates budgets at set intervals with rollover

startsOn.description: Date when the contract starts (deal starts recurring)

endsOn.description: Date when the contract ends (deal stops recurring)

nextOccurrenceOn.description: Date when the next deal will regenerate

template.description: Deal the contract is associated with

copyPurchaseOrderNumber.description: Setting to copy the purchase order number to the new deal on each recurrence

copyExpenses.description: Setting to copy the expenses to the new deal on each recurrence

useRolloverHours.description: Setting to use the rolled over hours from the previous deal on the new deal on each recurrence

custom_field_options.description: Custom Field Option is a selectable choice for a single-select (dropdown) or multi-select custom field. Each option belongs to a specific custom field and has a display name, position for ordering, and optional color for visual distinction. Options can be archived when no longer needed — archived options remain on existing records but are hidden from new selections.
custom_field_options.descriptionShort: selectable options for a dropdown or multi-select custom fields

custom_field_sections.description: Custom Field Section groups related custom fields together for visual organization. Sections create logical categories (e.g., "Financial Details", "Client Info") on detail views of tasks, deals, projects, and other customizable resources. Each custom field can belong to one section.

custom_fields.description:
Custom Field adds user-defined data to resources like tasks, deals, budgets, projects, people, companies, invoices, expenses, bookings, and services.
Each field has a customizable_type that determines which resource it extends, and a data_type that determines the input format:
text, number, select (single dropdown), multiselect, date, person reference, or file attachment.

Custom fields can be:
- Global: applies organization-wide to all instances of the target resource type.
- Project-scoped: applies only within a specific project (for task custom fields).
- Required: a value must be provided when creating or updating the resource.
- Sensitive: visible only to users with sensitive field access permission.

Select and multiselect fields have associated custom field options that define the available choices.
Fields are organized into custom field sections for visual grouping.
Fields can be archived when no longer needed.
custom_fields.descriptionShort: user-defined fields (text, number, dropdown, date, person, file) on tasks, deals, people, and other resources

global.description: Defines if the custom field is global (not bound to a project)

required.description: Defines if the custom field value is required on the object

sensitive.description: Is the custom field value sensitive (visible only to people with a sensitive access permission)?

project.description: Project the custom field is scoped to (if not global)

aggregationTypeId.description: Aggregation method used for number custom fields (e.g. sum, average)

formattingTypeId.description: Display formatting for number custom fields (e.g. plain number, percentage, currency)

survey.description: Survey this custom field belongs to (for survey-type custom fields)

deal_statuses.description:
Deal Status (Pipeline Stage) represents a named stage within a Pipeline that deals progress through.
Each deal status belongs to exactly one pipeline and has an ordered position within it.

Each status has a stage status that determines the deal outcome when moved to it:
- Open (status_id=1): deal is active and in progress.
- Won (status_id=2): deal was successfully closed. Probability defaults to 100%.
- Lost (status_id=3): deal was not successful. Probability defaults to 0%. Can require a lost reason (lost_reason_enabled).
- Delivered (status_id=4): budget work has been completed.

Per-stage configuration:
- Probability (0-100%): default win probability for revenue forecasting when a deal enters this stage.
- Tracking toggles: time tracking, expense tracking, and booking creation can be enabled/disabled per stage.
- The stage status cannot be changed after creation, as it would affect all existing deals in that status.
deal_statuses.descriptionShort: pipeline stages that define deal outcome (open/won/lost/delivered) and tracking capabilities

position.description: Position in the pipeline which indicates the lifecycle phase of the deal

statusId.description: Whether deals in this pipeline stage are considered open (in progress), won (closed successfully), lost (closed unsuccessfully), or delivered (work completed)

probabilityEnabled.description: Enables tracking of the probabiltiy of the deal of being won in this status

probability.description: Probability of the deal of being won in this stage

lostReasonEnabled.description: When enabled, a lost reason must be provided before a deal can be moved to this stage

timeTrackingEnabled.description: Whether time tracking is enabled for deals in this stage

expenseTrackingEnabled.description: Whether expense tracking is enabled for deals in this stage

bookingTrackingEnabled.description: Whether booking (resource planning) is enabled for deals in this stage

used.description: Whether this status is currently used by any deals

deals.description:
Deal represents a potential or actual job with a client company.
Deals are a polymorphic resource with two subtypes (differentiated by stage_type):
- Sales Deals (budget=false) — track opportunities through pipeline stages with win probability for revenue forecasting. Time can be tracked for presales work but is not billable until the deal is won.
- Production Budgets (budget=true) — active jobs where work is delivered, tracked, and billed. Budgets can be client-facing (billable) or internal (non-billable).

Deals contain Services — line items that define deliverables with billing types (Fixed, Time & Materials, Non-billable), pricing, quantity, and where time entries and expenses are tracked.
Services can be organized into Sections and can be moved between deals/budgets.
Deals can be linked to Projects for task management and can be recurring (retainer) through Contracts, which auto-generate new budget occurrences at set intervals.
Financial tracking includes revenue, cost (labor + expenses), profit, and margin. Invoicing is only possible from budgets, not from sales deals directly.
deals.descriptionShort: sales deals or production budgets — tracks pipeline opportunities or deliverables and billable work

close.name: close
close.description: Close a budget (sets closed_at to now).

open.name: open
open.description: Re-open a previously closed budget (clears closed_at).

budget.description: Whether the record is a sales deal or a production budget

stageType.description: Filter by whether the record is a sales deal or a production budget

company.description: Client company the deal or budget is associated with. Company cannot be internal company for deals. Budget can have either client or internal company.

project.description: Project associated with the deal or budget, if any. Only budgets can have associated internal projects. Deals can only have client projects

dealStatus.description: Current stage of the deal in its pipeline. Sales deals move through sales pipeline stages (e.g. Lead, Proposal, Won). Budgets have their own pipeline stages.

date.description: Date when the work on the deal should start (or started)

endDate.description: Date when the work on the deal should end (or ended)

probability.description: Estimated chance (0-100%) of winning this deal, used for revenue forecasting. Only relevant for sales deals, not budgets.

suffix.description: Suffix indicating the period if the deal is auto-recurring (used for retainer deals)

editorConfig.description: Simple editor configuration. When editorConfig.enabled is true, the deal uses the simple (guided) editor which constrains service configurations. Set editorConfig.enabled to false to convert to advanced editor mode.

contract.description: Contract the deal is associated with, if the deal is a retainer deal

lost_reason.description: Reason why the deal was lost. Required when setting a deal status that has lostReasonEnabled=true. Check the target deal status's lostReasonEnabled property - if true, you must provide a lost reason before submitting.

pipeline.description: Sales pipeline this deal belongs to

contact.description: Contact person at the client company for this deal

tagList.description: Tags assigned to the deal for categorization

lostComment.description: Comment explaining why the deal was lost

purchaseOrderNumber.description: Client purchase order number reference for invoicing

colorId.description: Color identifier for visual categorization in pipeline views

closedAt.description: When the budget was operationally closed (work completed). Null if still open. Distinct from sales_closed_on which tracks pipeline closure.

deliveredOn.description: Date when the work/deliverable was delivered to the client

salesClosedOn.description: Date when the deal was closed from a sales perspective (won or lost). Distinct from closed_at which tracks operational budget closure.

salesStatusUpdatedAt.description: When the deal last moved between pipeline stages

lastActivityAt.description: When the last activity (update, comment, status change) occurred on the deal

revenue.description: Total deal revenue in cents, aggregated from services

cost.description: Total deal cost in cents (work cost + expenses)

profit.description: Deal profit in cents (revenue minus cost)

profitMargin.description: Profit as a percentage of revenue (0-100 scale, e.g. 75.5 means 75.5%)

budgetTotal.description: Total budget amount allocated in cents

budgetUsed.description: Budget amount consumed so far in cents

workedTime.description: Total tracked time in minutes across all time entries

estimatedTime.description: Sum of estimated time from all services in minutes (bottom-up estimate of how long work will take)

budgetedTime.description: Top-down budgeted/planned time allocation in minutes (how much time was sold or allocated)

billableTime.description: Total billable time tracked in minutes (subset of worked time that is billable)

projectedRevenue.description: Revenue weighted by deal probability in cents (revenue * probability/100)

pendingInvoicing.description: Revenue recognized but not yet invoiced in cents

previousProbability.description: Win probability before the most recent change (0-100 scale)

daysSinceLastActivity.description: Number of days since the last activity on the deal

daysInCurrentStage.description: Number of days the deal has been in its current pipeline stage

daysSinceCreated.description: Number of days since the deal was created (or days from creation to sales close for closed deals)

timeApproval.description: Whether time entries on this budget require approval before they count

expenseApproval.description: Whether expenses on this budget require approval

budgetWarning.description: Budget warning threshold percentage (0-100). Triggers a notification when budget usage exceeds this value.

trackingTypeId.description: Controls who can track time: all services, only assigned services, or only assigned service types

status.description: Filter by operational budget status: open (work ongoing) or closed (work complete)

stageStatusId.description: Filter by deal stage status: open, won, or lost. Applies to both sales deals and budgets.

salesStatusId.description: Filter by sales pipeline status: open, won, or lost

needsClosing.description: Filter for budgets past their end date that have not been closed yet

needsInvoicing.description: Filter for closed budgets that still have uninvoiced revenue

recurring.description: Filter for recurring deals (linked to a contract) vs one-off deals

budgetUsage.description: Filter by budget consumption percentage (0-100 scale, e.g. 75 means 75% of budget used)

budgetRemaining.description: Filter by remaining budget amount in cents (budget_total minus budget_used)

deals.queryHints:
Deals is a polymorphic resource with subtypes: sales deals and budgets.
Each subtype has a different default filter scope:
- sales deals: { stage_type: {eq: '1'}, stage_status: {eq: '1'} }
- budgets: { stage_type: {eq: '2'}, status: {eq: '1'} }
When users ask about "deals" without specifying a subtype, the default is sales deals.
Users typically refer to production budgets just as "budgets".
The default filter scope is open deals and open budgets, unless the user specifies otherwise.

deleted_items.description: A record of a soft-deleted resource in the recycle bin. Deleted items track what was deleted, when, by whom, and can be restored to recover the original resource. Supported item types include: deal, project, budget, invoice, task, service, dashboard, time_entry, purchase_order, page, booking, event, expense, template, survey.
deleted_items.descriptionShort: soft-deleted and restoreable records in the recycle bin

restore.name: restore
restore.description: Restore a deleted item from the recycle bin, recovering the original resource and all its associated data.

location.description: Where the item was located (e.g., deal/budget name for services)

itemId.description: ID of the original deleted resource

itemType.description: Type of the deleted resource (e.g., service, task, project, deal, invoice)

recoveredAt.description: When the item was restored (null if not yet restored)

discussions.description: Discussion is a threaded conversation attached to a doc page. Discussions enable collaborative review by allowing comments on specific parts of a document. Each discussion has a status (active or resolved), can be reopened after resolution, and supports subscriber notifications. Discussions belong to a specific page.
discussions.descriptionShort: threaded conversations on a doc page for collaborative review

excerpt.description: Preview excerpt of the discussion content

subscriberIds.description: IDs of people subscribed to this discussion

page.description: Doc page this discussion belongs to

document_types.description:
Document Type defines the template and settings for generating PDF documents.
Four document types: Invoice (also used for credit notes), Proposal (for deals and budgets), Budget Status, and Purchase Order.

Each document type controls:
- Content: which fields appear (totals, line item details, attachments, notes, footer), their order, and layout.
- Localization: language and date format for the document.
- Visual style: linked to a Document Style that defines fonts, colors, headers/footers, and logo placement.
- Default tax rates: up to two tax components (e.g. federal + state) that pre-fill on documents.
- Subsidiary: determines which legal entity's branding and details appear.

Document types can be set as defaults per subsidiary or per client company.
Selection priority for invoices: client company default → subsidiary default → first template alphabetically.
Document types can be archived when outdated.
document_types.descriptionShort: templates for PDF generation of financial documents — content, layout, and branding

status.description: Whether the document type is active or archived

locale.description: Language/locale setting for the document type

document_types.queryHints: Users typically care about active document types, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for document types should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

einvoice_configurations.description:
EInvoiceConfiguration holds e-invoicing settings for a subsidiary. It includes flags for
enabling different e-invoice formats (FACE, KSeF, XRechnung, MER) and a kpdCodeMapping
which maps service type IDs to KPD code IDs for automatic KPD code assignment on Croatian
e-invoices. When a line item's service type has a KPD code mapped in the subsidiary's
e-invoice configuration, that KPD code can be automatically applied.

faceEnabled.description: Whether Croatian FACE e-invoicing is enabled

ksefEnabled.description: Whether Polish KSeF e-invoicing is enabled

xrechnungEnabled.description: Whether German XRechnung e-invoicing is enabled

kpdCodeMapping.description: Maps service type IDs to KPD code IDs for automatic KPD code assignment on line items (e.g. {"123": "456"} means service type 123 maps to KPD code 456)

includePdfAttachment.description: Whether to include a PDF attachment when sending e-invoices

subsidiary.description: The subsidiary this e-invoice configuration belongs to

email_threads.description: A conversation thread from a connected Gmail or Outlook inbox. Supports browsing, searching, listing, reading, archiving, moving, and managing email threads. Threads can be linked to Productive resources like deals, projects, and tasks.
email_threads.descriptionShort: user's live Gmail or Outlook inbox — browse, search, read, and manage emails

externalId.description: External provider thread ID (Gmail thread ID or Outlook conversation ID)

participants.description: Thread participants — array of {type: "To"|"From"|"Cc"|"Bcc", name: string, address: string}

receivedAt.description: When the last email in the thread was received

hasAttachments.description: Whether any email in the thread has attachments

unread.description: Whether the thread has unread emails

linkedType.description: Type of linked Productive resource (e.g. Deal, Project)

integration.description: The email integration this thread belongs to. Auto-included by the API — do not request via include param.

emails.description: Individual emails in this thread. Auto-included by the API — do not request via include param.

query.description: Search query passed to the native email provider search API. See the email-inbox guide for syntax details.

emails.description:
Email is a record synced from external email providers (Gmail, Outlook) or received via BCC forwarding.
Emails can be linked to contacts, deals, budgets, invoices, projects, and tasks for relationship tracking and communication history.

Emails are organized in threads and appear in the activity feed of linked items.
Read/unread status syncs bidirectionally with the email provider.
Emails can be auto-linked to deals based on contact matching, or manually linked by users.

Lifecycle: unresolved → resolved or dismissed. Mostly read-only — email content comes from external sources.
Emails can be marked as read or dismissed via actions.

Note: This resource represents emails linked to CRM records.
Gmail and Outlook inbox thread browsing and management (listing, searching, archiving,
moving) is handled by the email_threads resource.
emails.descriptionShort: emails synced from external providers, linked to contacts, deals, projects, and tasks

body.description: Email body content (plain text). May be empty for Outlook emails — use bodyHtml instead.

bodyHtml.description: Email body content (HTML). Primary body field for Outlook emails where body (plain text) is empty.

snippet.description: Short preview snippet of the email body

autoLinked.description: Whether the email was automatically linked to a deal

outgoing.description: Whether this is an outgoing (sent) email

deal.description: Deal this email is linked to

invoice.description: Invoice this email is linked to

entitlements.description:
Entitlement represents a time-off allocation for a person on a specific absence category (event) for a defined period.
Only applicable to absence categories with a limited limitation type (limited by days or hours).

Each entitlement tracks:
- Allocated: total days or hours the person is entitled to (e.g. 21 vacation days). Can include half-day allocations (e.g. 4.5 days).
- Used: days/hours consumed through approved absence bookings (auto-calculated).
- Pending: days/hours in bookings awaiting approval (auto-calculated).
- Available balance: allocated - used - pending.

Entitlements are scoped to a date range (start_date to end_date), typically a calendar year.
A person must have an entitlement allocated before they can request time off for that absence category.
Holidays (from the person's holiday calendar) are excluded from entitlement deductions — booking over a holiday does not reduce the balance.
entitlements.descriptionShort: time-off balance for a person — allocated, used, and pending

startDate.description: Start date of the entitlement period

endDate.description: End date of the entitlement period

allocated.description: Total allocated days or hours for this entitlement

used.description: Days or hours already used through approved bookings (auto-calculated)

note.description: Optional note or description for this entitlement

person.description: Person this entitlement is allocated to

events.description:
Absence Category (Event) defines a type of time off or remote work that people can be booked on in the Resource Planner.
Configured by admins. Examples: Vacation, Sick Leave, Parental Leave, Remote Work.

Each category is configured with:
- Absence type: time_off (person is away, reduces availability for project work) or remote_work (person works remotely, does not affect availability).
- Compensation: paid (event_type=1, included in internal cost calculations) or unpaid (event_type=2).
- Limitation type: limited by days (2), limited by hours (3), or unlimited (4). Limited categories require Entitlements to define each person's annual quota.

People are booked on absence categories via absence Bookings (bookings with event_id set).
Absence bookings can go through an approval workflow if absence approval is enabled for the organization.
Categories can sync status to Slack and external calendars when status sync is enabled.
Categories can be archived when no longer needed.
events.descriptionShort: absence categories (e.g. vacation) with time-off type, compensation, and usage limits
events.queryHints: Users typically care about active absence categories, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

expense_line_items.description: A line item on an expense, with a name, quantity, and unit price. Individual line items are managed via bulk operations on this resource. The expense_line_items field on the parent expense replaces all line items on every write and cannot target individual ones.
expense_line_items.descriptionShort: expense line items with name, quantity, and unit price

unitPrice.description: Unit price as a money object, e.g. {amount: "100.00", currency: "USD"}

unitPriceAmount.description: Unit price in cents (e.g. 50000 = 500.00). Immutable after the expense is paid.

taxRate.description: Tax rate applied to this line item

expense.description: The expense this line item belongs to

expenses.description:
Expense is a non-labor cost logged against a Service in a Deal or Budget (e.g. travel, accommodation, software licenses, subcontractors, stock purchases).
Expenses can only be tracked on services with tracking unit set to Piece and expense tracking enabled.

Each expense has several independent statuses:
- Approval: unapproved → approved or rejected. Only approved expenses affect revenue and can be invoiced. Approval can be required via policies on budgets/deals.
- Payment: unpaid or paid (with pay_on due date and paid_on actual date).
- Invoicing: not invoiced → drafted → finalized. Billable expenses can be included on client invoices.
- Reimbursement: not reimbursable, requested, or reimbursed. Tracks whether the employee who incurred the expense gets paid back.

Revenue impact depends on the service's billing type:
- T&M by piece: approved expenses generate revenue (billable_amount × quantity).
- Fixed by piece: approved expenses assume (transfer) revenue from the service's fixed total.
- Non-billable: expenses generate cost only, never revenue.

Expenses can be linked to Purchase Orders for vendor management and financial compliance.
Billable amount can differ from cost amount via a markup percentage.

Constraints:
- A purchase_order can only be linked to an expense with a single line item (or no line items).
- unit_price and quantity on a line item are immutable once the expense is paid.
- Line items are immutable on approved expenses.
expenses.descriptionShort: non-labor costs on a service (e.g. software) — with approval, invoicing, and reimbursement workflows

payOn.description: Date when the expense payment is due

paidOn.description: Date when the expense was paid

position.description: Position of the expense in the deal

invoiced.description: Is the expense invoiced to the client?

approved.description: Is the expense approved by the responsible person?

approvedAt.description: Date when the expense was approved

rejected.description: Is the expense rejected by the responsible person?

rejectedAt.description: Date when the expense was rejected

reimbursable.description: Is the expense reimbursable to the employee?

reimbursedOn.description: Date when the expense was reimbursed

quantity.description: Quantity of the expense (not needed when using expense_line_items)

amount.description: Total expense cost in cents. Required when not using expense_line_items (simple mode). Auto-calculated from line items when expense_line_items are provided and should not be set in that case.

billableAmount.description: Amount of the expense that is billable to the client

expenseLineItems.description: Write-only. Replaces all existing line items when provided. Each item requires name, unit_price (cents), and quantity. Amount is calculated automatically.

lineItems.description: Current line items on this expense (read-only). Writes go through the expense_line_items attribute or via the expense_line_items resource directly.

vendor.description: Vendor company the expense is for

service.description: Service the expense is tracked on

purchaseOrder.description: Purchase order this expense is linked to

invoiceAttribution.description: Invoice this expense is attributed to

taxInclusion.description: Whether the expense amount includes tax

markup.description: Markup percentage applied to calculate the billable amount from the cost amount

amountWithTax.description: Expense amount including tax in cents

totalAmountWithTax.description: Total amount including tax in cents (quantity * amount_with_tax)

recognizedRevenue.description: Revenue recognized for this expense in cents

exchangeRate.description: Exchange rate to organization default currency

companyId.description: Filter expenses by client company (via deal)

responsibleId.description: Filter expenses by deal responsible person

status.description: Filter by payment status: paid or unpaid

invoicingStatus.description: Filter by invoicing workflow status: not invoiced, drafted, or finalized

approvalStatus.description: Filter by approval status: approved, unapproved, rejected, or auto-approved

reimbursement.description: Filter by reimbursement status: none, requested, or reimbursed

filters.description:
View (internally called Filter) is a saved configuration of filters, groupers, sorters, and visible fields.
This resource serves two purposes:
- Views: saved perspectives on list screens throughout the application (e.g. "My open tasks", "Overdue budgets", "Design team bookings"). Scoped to a filterable_collection (tasks, deals, people, bookings, etc.).
- Reports: saved analytical reports built from data sources (e.g. time entries, budgets, invoices, financial items). Reports support aggregations, charts, and can be added to dashboards as widgets.

Both views and reports store their configuration in the params field.
They can be private (visible only to the creator) or shared with other users via memberships, with "Can view" or "Full access" levels.
Reports can be organized into custom categories for easier navigation.

filterableCollection.description: Type of objects the view is made for (e.g. "tasks", "deals", "people", etc.)

folders.description:
Folder (also called Board) is a container for grouping Task Lists within a Project.
A project can have multiple folders to organize work into logical sections (e.g. sprints, phases, work streams, priorities).
Each folder contains one or more task lists, and each task list contains tasks.

Folders can be reordered within a project, moved to another project (with all tasks and task lists), duplicated, and archived.
Archiving a folder does not change the status of tasks inside it — tasks may still appear in reports unless separately closed.
Tasks within folders can be viewed in List, Board, Table, Calendar, Timeline, or Gantt layouts.
folders.descriptionShort: collections of task lists within a project (e.g. sprints, phases)
folders.queryHints: Users typically care about active folders, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for folders should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

holiday_calendars.description:
Holiday Calendar defines a set of public/bank holidays for a specific country or region.
Configured by admins. Each organization can have multiple calendars for different regions or subsidiaries.

People are assigned to a holiday calendar through their cost rate (salary) records.
Holidays in the assigned calendar are treated as non-working days — they reduce the person's capacity and availability across Productive:
- Resource Planner: bookings over holidays don't count hours.
- Time tracking: holidays are marked as non-working; time can still be logged manually.
- Absence booking: holidays are excluded from time-off allowance deductions.
- Workload layout: capacity shows as zero on holidays.

When autogenerate_holidays is enabled, holidays are pre-filled and updated based on the selected country and state.
When disabled, holidays must be added manually. If a state is not selected, only nationwide holidays are included.
holiday_calendars.descriptionShort: set of holidays for a country that affects availability

holidays.description:
Holiday is a specific non-working day within a Holiday Calendar (e.g. Christmas, New Year, Independence Day).
Each holiday has a name and date, and belongs to exactly one holiday calendar.

Holidays reduce availability and capacity to zero for that day for all people assigned to the parent calendar.
They are excluded from absence allowance calculations — booking time off over a holiday does not deduct from the person's entitlement.

Holidays can be auto-generated by the parent calendar (based on country/state) or created manually.
If an auto-generated holiday is deleted, it will not reappear unless the calendar is recreated.
holidays.descriptionShort: non-working day within a holiday calendar

after.description: Filter holidays on or after this date

before.description: Filter holidays on or before this date

integrations.description: External service connected to the organization. Calendar integrations (Google Calendar, Outlook Calendar) sync events; email integrations (Gmail, Outlook Mail) power the inbox — realm_id holds the connected email address.
integrations.descriptionShort: connected external services (Gmail, Google Calendar, Outlook, Slack, Xero, etc.)

realmId.description: Account identifier — for email integrations this is the email address of the connected account

deactivatedAt.description: When the integration was deactivated (null = active)

calendars.description: List of calendars available from this integration

lastSyncedAt.description: When the integration last synced data

invoice_attributions.description:
Invoice Attribution is a junction record linking an Invoice to a Budget (deal with budget=true), specifying what portion of the invoice amount is attributed to that budget.

A single invoice can span multiple budgets — each attribution defines the amount allocated to one budget.
This enables:
- Split invoicing: one invoice covers work from several budgets for the same client.
- Budget invoicing tracking: the attributed amount feeds into the budget's "invoiced" and "pending_invoicing" calculations.

Optionally scoped to a date range (date_from, date_to) for time-based billing periods (e.g. monthly T&M invoicing).
The currency is inherited from the invoice and is read-only on the attribution.
invoice_attributions.descriptionShort: links between invoices and budgets which define the attributed amount

dateFrom.description: Start of the billing period for this attribution

dateTo.description: End of the billing period for this attribution

amount.description: Amount attributed to this budget in cents

budget.description: Deal/budget this invoice amount is attributed to

invoices.description:
Invoice is a financial document sent to a client for payment.
Invoices can only be created from budgets (not directly from sales deals). They contain Line Items detailing the billed work.

Two document types via invoice_type: regular invoice (1) or credit note (2).
Credit notes reverse or adjust a previous invoice (linked via parent_invoice) and have negative line item quantities.

Invoice lifecycle: draft → finalized → sent → paid.
- Draft: freely editable, no invoice number yet. Line items can be generated from uninvoiced time & expenses, total/remaining budget amount, or a percentage of total budget.
- Finalized: invoice number assigned, locked by default. Only users with edit-finalized permission can unlock. Currency is locked on the budget once the first invoice (even draft) exists.
- Sent: marked as sent (manually or via email from Productive). Payment reminders can be added. Cannot be edited unless marked as unsent first.
- Paid: fully paid (tracked via Payments). Partial payments are possible — amount_paid, amount_unpaid, and amount_written_off track the payment state.

Each invoice belongs to a client company and subsidiary (issuing legal entity).
Invoices are linked to one or more budgets via Invoice Attributions, which specify how much of the invoice amount is attributed to each budget.
Supports tax calculations per line item, discounts, purchase order numbers, multi-currency, tags, custom fields, and export to accounting tools.
invoices.descriptionShort: billing documents — draft → finalized → sent → paid — with line items from budget services

invoiceTypeId.description: Whether this is a regular invoice or a credit note

sentOn.description: Date the invoice was sent to the client

paidOn.description: Date the invoice was fully paid

exported.description: Whether the invoice has been exported to an accounting system

credited.description: Whether a credit note has been applied to this invoice

paymentTerms.description: Number of days between invoice date and due date

billTo.description: Billing recipient contact data — contains country, address, VAT of the client being billed

billFrom.description: Billing sender contact data — contains country, address, VAT of the issuing subsidiary

kpd_codes.description:
KPD (Klasifikacija Proizvoda po Djelatnostima) codes are product classification codes
used in Croatian e-invoicing. Each code classifies a product or service for tax and
regulatory purposes. KPD codes (8-character) are specific product codes, while NKD codes
(shorter) are broader activity classifications. These codes are assigned to invoice line
items for e-invoice compliance.
kpd_codes.descriptionShort: Croatian product classification codes for e-invoicing compliance

isKpd.description: True for KPD codes (8-char), false for NKD codes (shorter)

line_items.description:
Line Item is an individual charge entry on an Invoice.
Each line item has a description, quantity, unit (hour/day/piece), unit price, optional discount, and a tax rate.
Amounts (net, tax, total) are computed server-side from quantity × unit_price - discount + tax.

Line items can be generated in three ways:
- From uninvoiced time and expenses: pulls approved, uninvoiced time entries and expenses from the budget's services.
- From total/remaining budget amount: creates line items for the full or remaining budget value.
- From a percentage of the total budget: invoices a fraction of the budget.

Line items can be linked to a service and/or expense for traceability back to the budget.
Descriptions support rich text and dynamic fields (e.g. person name, task title, period) for flexible presentation.
Line items can be grouped by service, budget, project, or task on the invoice.
Currency is inherited from the parent invoice.
line_items.descriptionShort: invoice lines with quantity, unit price, tax, and optional link to a service or expense

taxName.description: Name of the applied tax (derived from tax rate)

currency.description: Currency inherited from the parent invoice

invoice.description: Parent invoice this line item belongs to

service.description: Service (billing point) this line item was generated from

expense.description: Expense this line item was generated from

taxRate.description: Tax rate applied to this line item

kpdCode.description: KPD classification code for Croatian e-invoicing

lost_reasons.description:
Lost Reason is a configurable label explaining why a deal was lost (e.g. "Price too high", "Went with competitor", "Unrealistic timeline").
When a deal moves to a pipeline stage with lost_reason_enabled=true, selecting a lost reason becomes mandatory.
Organizations define their own set of lost reasons for sales reporting and win/loss analysis.
lost_reasons.descriptionShort: labels explaining why a deal was lost

memberships.description:
Membership controls who can access a target resource (project, doc, dashboard, view, survey, or agent).
Access can be granted to a person (type_id=1), a dynamic group (type_id=2), or a team (type_id=3).

Access levels vary by target type:
- Projects: member (access_type=5) — grants access to tasks, docs, and budgets within the project.
- Docs (pages): full (1), write (2), read (3), or comment (4).
- Agents: full (1) or read (3).
- Views/Dashboards/Surveys: full (1) or read (3).

Dynamic groups are predefined: employees (2), project members (6), project manager (8), deal owner (9), users that can manage project (10), agent manager (12).
If someone cannot be assigned to a task or track time, they likely need a membership on that project first.
memberships.descriptionShort: access controls for projects, docs, views, dashboards, surveys, and agents

accessTypeId.description: Available levels depend on target resource type. Filter/Dashboard/Survey/Agent: [full, read]. Quote/Workspace: [member]. Others: [full, write, read, comment].

targetId.description: ID of the polymorphic target resource.

targetType.description: Type of the target resource (Workspace, Page, Dashboard, Filter, Quote, Pulse, Surveys::Survey, Agents::Agent).

person.description: Required when membership_type=1. Person receiving access

team.description: Required when membership_type=3. Team receiving access

project.description: Required when giving access to a project.

page.description: Required when giving access to a doc.

agent.description: Required when giving access to an agent.

notifications.description: Notification represents an inbox item for the user. Notifications track changes to tasks, deals, pages, and other objects. Each notification has a target object, unread activity count, and can be marked as read, dismissed, or restored.

undismiss.name: undismiss
undismiss.description: Restore a dismissed notification back to inbox

targetId.description: ID of the target object (task, deal, page, etc.)

targetType.description: Type of the target object (Task, Deal, Page, etc.)

read.description: Whether the notification has been read

dismissed.description: Whether the notification has been dismissed

newActivitiesCount.description: Number of unread activities on this notification

excerpt.description: Short preview text of the notification content

lastActionAt.description: Timestamp of the last action on the notification

firstUnreadActivityId.description: ID of the first unread activity

madeByAutomation.description: Whether the notification was triggered by an automation

after.description: Load notifications with last_action_at after this datetime

before.description: Load notifications with last_action_at before this datetime

actor.description: The person who triggered the notification

owner.description: The organization membership that owns this notification

notifications.queryHints: Users typically care about active (non-dismissed) notifications they are subscribed to (is_important=true), unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for notifications should be: { is_dismissed: {eq: 'false'}, is_important: {eq: 'true'} }.

organization_memberships.description: Organization Membership links a user to an organization, granting them access. Each membership connects a person (profile) and a user (login account) to a specific organization. A user can be a member of multiple organizations.
organization_memberships.descriptionShort: links between users and organizations, granting access

organization_subscriptions.description: Organization Subscription holds the current pricing plan and product entitlements for an organization. Exposes which subscription features are available (switch on/off) or metered (with usage limits). Read-only — subscriptions are managed through billing.
organization_subscriptions.descriptionShort: pricing plan and feature entitlements for an organizations

organizations.description: Organization is the top-level account in Productive. It represents a company that uses Productive to manage its business operations. An organization owns all other data — projects, deals, people, invoices, etc. It defines global settings: currency, time zone, fiscal year, working hours, locale, and date/number formats. Organizations contain subsidiaries (legal entities), service types, workflows, pipelines, rate cards, tax rates, and custom fields.

fiscalYearStart.description: Date when the fiscal year starts

releaseChannel.description: Release channel for feature flags: stable, beta, or alpha

currency.description: Currency used by default for new deals, budgets, and financial operations

workingHours.description: Default working hours for each day of the week (Mon-Sun)

manDayMinutes.description: Number of minutes in a man-day, used for converting time to man-days in reports

locale.description: Default locale for the organization (e.g. en, de, fr)

dateFormatId.description: Date display format preference for the organization

numberFormatId.description: Number display format preference (e.g. decimal/thousand separators)

pages.description:
Page is a rich text document in a hierarchical structure. A page is either a root page (what users call a "Doc") or a child page nested under a parent within the same Doc.
- Root page (Doc): has no parent_page_id or root_page_id. Can be linked to a Project or standalone. Owns all sharing and access settings — child pages inherit them.
- Child page: must set both parent_page_id and root_page_id on creation. Nested arbitrarily deep.

Sharing is controlled at the Doc (root page) level via Memberships:
- Access levels: view, comment, edit, or full access. Can be granted to individuals or teams.
- Project docs are visible to project members by default; standalone docs are visible only to their creator.
- Docs can also be shared publicly via a read-only web link (public_access).

Pages support rich formatting (ProseMirror schema): tables, lists, checklists, banners, embedded files/images, and cover images.
Pages can be moved between docs or projects, and child pages can be converted to docs and vice versa.
pages.descriptionShort: rich text pages — root (Docs) with sharing, or child pages in a Doc hierarchy

editedAt.description: Last time the page content was edited

publicAccess.description: Whether the page is accessible via a public shared link

public.description: Whether the page is visible to all organization members

rootPage.description: Root page (top-level Doc) of the hierarchy. Required together with parent_page when creating a child page — both must be set or both omitted.

parentPage.description: Direct parent page in the hierarchy. Required together with root_page when creating a child page — both must be set or both omitted.

payments.description:
Payment records money received against an invoice, or an amount written off as uncollectable.
The invoice must be marked as sent before payments can be recorded.

Two types of payment records:
- Payment (paid_on set): actual money received from the client. Reduces the invoice's amount_unpaid.
- Write-off (written_off_on set): forgiven debt that won't be collected. Reduces amount_unpaid and increases amount_written_off on the invoice.

Multiple payments can be recorded against a single invoice for partial payments.
When total payments + write-offs equal the invoice amount_with_tax, the invoice status changes to "Paid".
Payments can be synced one-way from accounting tools (Xero, QuickBooks, Exact, E-conomic) — synced payments cannot be deleted in Productive.
Currency is inherited from the invoice and is read-only.
payments.descriptionShort: money received or written off against an invoices

paidOn.description: Date when the payment was received (required unless write-off)

writtenOffOn.description: Date when the amount was written off (alternative to paid_on)

people.description:
Person is the central resource in Productive — most other resources reference people as assignees, creators, approvers, or responsible parties.
People is a polymorphic resource with five subtypes, distinguished by hrm_type and person_type:

1. Employees (hrm_type=1, person_type=1):
Full organization members with login access. Count as paid seats.
Can be assigned to tasks, track time, log expenses, be booked on services in the Resource Planner, and request time off.
Have cost rates (salaries) that determine hourly cost for profitability tracking, working capacity, and holiday calendar.
Assigned to teams, subsidiaries, and service types. Have a manager for org chart hierarchy.
Permissions are controlled via system or custom permission sets.

2. Contractors (hrm_type=1, person_type=1):
External collaborators with login access, employed by a different company. Count as paid seats.
Same capabilities as employees (tasks, time tracking, bookings, cost rates) but linked to an external company.
Overhead is typically disabled on their cost rates since they don't generate organizational overhead.

3. Clients (hrm_type=2, person_type=1):
External users invited to collaborate on specific projects. Free seats (no cost).
Client Staff: can view and manage tasks, comment, and collaborate on docs within shared projects.
Client Managers: additionally can view budgets and a client-facing subset of financial data (services, time entries, expenses) on budgets where client access is enabled.
Cannot access profitability data, cost rates, or internal projects. Custom client permissions can be created for tailored access.

4. Contacts (hrm_type=2, person_type=2):
CRM records without login access. Store contact details, notes, and linked emails.
Cannot collaborate in the app but can be converted to any user type (Employee, Contractor, or Client) at any time via the invite action.
Archived via archived_at (not deactivated_at which is for users).

5. Placeholders (person_type=3):
Named resource slots for capacity planning before a real person is hired or assigned.
Can have cost rates and be booked on services in the Resource Planner, but cannot be added to projects, assigned to tasks, or track time.
Confirmed placeholder bookings can be reassigned to a real person. Available on Professional (5) and Ultimate (10) plans.

People can be active (status=1) or deactivated/archived (status=2).
People can set custom status (emoji + text with expiration) that syncs to Slack and external calendars.
people.descriptionShort: employees, contractors, clients, contacts, or placeholders — with type-specific capabilities and access

invite.name: invite
invite.description: Invite a person to become a user with login access. Creates the associated User record and sends an invitation email.

title.description: Title or function in an organizational structure

avatarUrl.description: URL of the person's avatar image.

agent.description: Whether this person is an AI agent (not a human user).

contact.description: Contact information hash containing phone numbers, addresses, and other contact details. Only visible if the current user has permission to view contact information.

statusEmoji.description: Custom status emoji set by the person (e.g. vacation, focus mode).

statusText.description: Custom status text set by the person (e.g. "On vacation until Monday").

statusExpiresAt.description: When the custom status automatically expires.

lastSeenAt.description: Timestamp when the person was last active in Productive.

hrmTypeId.description: Determines whether the person is an employee/contractor or a CRM contact/client. Use this filter to query people by category.

isUser.description: Whether this person has a login account. Contacts (isUser=false) are listed in the CRM but cannot sign in.

projectWatching.description: Filter to find people who should be subscribed to new tasks in the project (aka "project watchers")

accessibleProjectId.description: Filter to find people who have access and can collaborate on the project(s)

subscribableType.description: Type of the resource the person is subscribed to

subscribableId.description: ID of the resource the person is subscribed to (used together with subscribable_type)

archivedAt.description: Date when the contact was archived (soft-deleted). Only applicable to contacts — employees/contractors use deactivated_at instead.

deactivatedAt.description: Date when the user was deactivated. Applicable to employees and contractors (users with login access). Contacts use archived_at instead.

virtual.description: Whether this is a virtual person (not a real individual). Virtual people are used for shared/pooled resource slots.

placeholder.description: Whether this is a placeholder person (unnamed resource slot used for capacity planning before a real person is assigned).

joinedAt.description: Date when the person joined the organization (employment start date)

availabilities.description: Serialized salary/engagement periods defining the person's working schedule. Array of tuples: [started_on, ended_on, working_hours, holiday_calendar_id]. working_hours is an array of 7 numbers (hours per weekday, Mon=0..Sun=6) or 14 numbers for alternating week schedules (week1 Mon..Sun, week2 Mon..Sun). ended_on is null for the current/open-ended engagement.

customRole.description: Permission set (role) assigned to the person

company.description: Company the person is associated with. For employees and contractors, this is usually the internal company. For clients, this is a client company.

subsidiary.description: Subsidiary (legal entity) the person belongs to. Used for financial reporting and overhead calculations.

teams.description: Teams the person is a member of.

autotracking.description: Whether automatic time tracking is enabled for this person

approvalPolicyAssignment.description: The approval policy assignment for this person — defines which absence approval policy governs their time-off requests

pipelines.description:
Pipeline defines the stages (Deal Statuses) that Deals move through.
Two types: sales (pipeline_type=1) for tracking opportunities from lead to close, and production (pipeline_type=2) for managing budgets.

Organizations can have multiple pipelines for different product lines, customer segments, or sales teams.
The number of pipelines depends on the subscription plan (Essential: 1, Professional: 3, Ultimate: 5).

Each pipeline contains ordered Deal Statuses (stages) with:
- A stage status: open, won, or lost. Moving a deal to a won/lost stage triggers the corresponding deal outcome.
- Default probability (0-100%) for revenue forecasting (Ultimate plan). Won stages default to 100%, lost to 0%.
- Per-stage toggles for time tracking, expense tracking, and booking creation on deals in that stage.
- Optional mandatory lost reason requirement when a deal enters a lost stage.

Configured by admins in sales settings.
pipelines.descriptionShort: deal stages — sales pipelines for opportunities or production pipelines for budgets

position.description: Order of the pipeline in the list, used for organizing multiple sales pipelines

prices.description:
Price is a line item on a Rate Card that serves as a service template.
When adding services to a deal/budget from a rate card, prices pre-fill the service configuration.

Each price defines:
- Service type, name, and description (template for the service).
- Billing type (Fixed, T&M, Non-billable) and tracking unit (hour, day, piece).
- Rate (price per unit), quantity, currency, discount, and markup.
- Tracking toggles: time tracking, expense tracking, booking tracking, and budget cap.
- Estimated hours for internal planning.

Prices belong to a Rate Card, which is either a company-wide default (linked to the organization's company) or a client-specific rate card (linked to a client company).
When both exist, client-specific prices appear alongside company defaults when working with that client's deals/budgets.
prices.descriptionShort: rate card line items with billing type, rate, unit, and tracking defaults

quantity.description: Quantity of pieces, hours or days that service is budgeting for

timeTrackingEnabled.description: Defines whether time tracking will be enabled on the service

expenseTrackingEnabled.description: Defines whether expense tracking will be enabled on the service

bookingTrackingEnabled.description: Defines whether booking tracking will be enabled on the service

budgetCapEnabled.description: Defines whether budget cap will be enabled on the service?

projects.description:
Project is a workspace where work is organized and tracked.
Two types: client (project_type=2) for billable client work, and internal (project_type=1) for non-billable overhead.

Projects contain:
- Folders and Task Lists for organizing tasks. Tasks follow the project's Workflow (configurable task statuses).
- Docs (Pages) for documentation, shared with project members by default.
- Budgets linked for financial tracking — client projects have billable budgets, internal projects only have non-billable budgets.
- Surveys (Forms) for collecting structured data from users, with automations that can create tasks from responses.

Projects can be viewed in List, Board, Table, Calendar, Timeline, or Gantt layouts.
Access is controlled via Memberships — project members, teams, and clients can be added with varying permissions.
Clients can collaborate on tasks in client projects. With the Client Manager permission and explicit budget access enabled, clients can also view a client-facing subset of financial data (services, time entries, expenses) on budgets they are invited to.
Internal projects cannot have clients added. Converting between client and internal is possible but resets billable data.
Projects can be archived when completed. Archived projects have restricted editing — only name, project manager, and custom field values can be updated. Other fields, memberships, folders, and task lists cannot be modified until the project is restored.
projects.descriptionShort: workspaces for tasks, docs, surveys, budgets and invoices — billable or internal (overhead)

archivedAt.description: When the project was archived (null if active)

lastActivityAt.description: When the last activity occurred on the project

company.description: Company (client) the project is for, or organization's company if the project is internal

projects.queryHints: Users typically care about active projects, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for projects should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

proposal_sections.description:
Proposal Section groups Proposal Services within a Proposal.
Sections are a snapshot of the deal's sections at the time of proposal creation or regeneration.
They organize proposal services into logical categories (e.g. by phase, department, or deliverable type).
Each section has a name, position, and optional editor configuration for rich text content.
Sections are read-only — changes require regenerating the proposal from the deal.
proposal_sections.descriptionShort: groups of proposal services which mirrors a deal section

editorConfig.description: Rich text editor configuration for the section content

proposal_services.description:
Proposal Service is a line item within a Proposal, representing a specific type of work with its billing details.
Proposal services are synced from the deal's services when the proposal is created or regenerated — they are a snapshot, not a live reference.

Each proposal service captures: name, description, service type, billing type (Fixed/T&M/Non-billable), unit, quantity, price, discount, markup, and computed totals (budget_total, revenue, discount_amount, markup_amount).
Services are grouped into Proposal Sections that mirror the deal's sections.
The origin_service_id links back to the deal service it was copied from.
Most fields are read-only — changes to the deal's services require regenerating the proposal to update.
proposal_services.descriptionShort: deal service snapshots in a proposal with billing type, quantity, price, and totals

quantity.description: Proposed quantity in pieces, hours, or days

discount.description: Discount percentage on the proposed service

markup.description: Markup percentage on the proposed service

originServiceId.description: ID of the deal service this proposal service was synced from

initialServiceId.description: ID of the initial deal service

billingTypeId.description: Proposed billing type: Fixed, Time and Materials, or Non-billable

budgetCapEnabled.description: Whether budget cap is enabled for the proposed service

budgetTotal.description: Total budget of the proposed service

proposals.description:
Proposal is a sales document that presents proposed work and pricing to a client for approval.
