Proposals are created on sales deals — each proposal is a snapshot of the deal's sections and services at the time of creation or last sync.

Lifecycle: draft → sent → accepted or rejected.
- Draft: editable, can be regenerated to sync with deal changes. Uses a document type for styling and layout.
- Sent: shared via email or public link. Generating a public link automatically sets status to sent.
- Accepted: client accepted (and optionally e-signed) via the public link. Signed proposals are locked and cannot be edited.
- Rejected: marked by the user in Productive. Disables the public link for signing.

E-signature support: clients can type, draw, or upload a signature. Acceptance is logged with an Audit PDF containing signer details, IP address, timestamp, and PDF fingerprint.
A disclaimer can be configured that clients must accept before signing.

Plan differences: Essential allows one PDF proposal per deal. Professional/Ultimate add public links and e-signature. Ultimate allows multiple proposals per deal plus analytics.
Proposals include a tax rate, purchase order number, custom fields, and note/footer with variable interpolation.
proposals.descriptionShort: sales quotes for deals — scope and pricing presentation with e-signature and approval

status.description: Proposal lifecycle status (draft, sent, accepted, rejected)

linkStatus.description: Whether the public link is active or inactive

sentAt.description: When the proposal was sent to the client

signed.description: Whether the proposal has been signed by the client

responderName.description: Name of the person who accepted the proposal

responderEmail.description: Email of the person who accepted the proposal

originalPdfUrl.description: URL of the original proposal PDF

auditPdfUrl.description: URL of the audit trail PDF

publicUuid.description: UUID for the public proposal link

client.description: Client company the proposal is for

documentType.description: Defines the structure, style, and content of the proposal document.

purchase_orders.description:
Purchase Order (PO) is a document sent to a vendor to order goods or services.
Each PO is tied to a single budget and cannot span multiple budgets. Available on the Ultimate plan.

PO lifecycle: draft → finalized → sent → received.
- Draft: expenses are created as draft line items against budget services (services must have unit=Piece with expense tracking enabled).
- Finalized: all linked expenses are also finalized, impacting the budget. A PO number is assigned.
- Sent: marked as sent to the vendor (manually or via email). Sent POs cannot be edited unless marked as unsent first.
- Received: tracked via Bills that record received quantities. Payment status updates automatically (not received → partially received → fully received).

Each expense line item on the PO is a separate Expense record linked to a service in the budget.
Expenses can be unlinked from a PO (keeping them in the budget) or deleted (removing from both).
Deleting a PO deletes all linked items unless unlinked first.
Supports multi-currency, vendor management, document templates, and export to accounting tools.
purchase_orders.descriptionShort: vendor procurement documents on a budget — line items become expenses on budget services

issuedOn.description: Date the purchase order was issued

sentOn.description: Date the purchase order was sent to the vendor

note.description: Internal notes on the purchase order

paymentStatusId.description: Payment/receipt status (not received, partially received, fully received)

sentStatus.description: Whether the purchase order has been sent

exported.description: Whether the purchase order has been exported to an accounting system

vendor.description: Vendor company this purchase order is sent to

deal.description: Deal/budget this purchase order belongs to

creator.description: Person who created the purchase order

rate_cards.description:
Rate Card is a reusable price list containing Prices (service templates) for Service Types.
Used when adding services to deals/budgets from a rate card to pre-fill service configuration.

Two scopes:
- Company rate card: linked to the organization's own company. Available for all client deals/budgets. Limited by plan (Professional: 10, Ultimate: 30).
- Client-specific rate card: linked to a client company. Shown only when working with that client. Unlimited count.

Rate cards can be archived when no longer needed — archived cards cannot be used for new deals/budgets but existing ones remain unchanged.
Rate cards can be bulk-updated via CSV export/import for price changes across many services at once.
rate_cards.descriptionShort: price lists for services — company-wide or client-specific pricing

company.description: Company (client, or organization's company for default rate cards) the rate card is for

resource_requests.description: Resource request (staffing request) represents a request to allocate a person to a service for a given date range. Lifecycle: pending → resolved, rejected, or canceled.
resource_requests.descriptionShort: staffing requests to allocate people to a service for a given date range

resolve.name: resolve
resolve.description: Resolves a resource request by creating bookings for the selected people and marking the request as resolved.

cancel.name: cancel
cancel.description: Cancels a pending resource request. No additional input required.

reject.name: reject
reject.description: Rejects a resource request. Requires a rejected_reason in the request body.

title.description: Title or label of the resource request

note.description: Additional notes for the request (free-text, HTML escaped)

startedOn.description: Start date of the requested allocation (YYYY-MM-DD)

endedOn.description: End date of the requested allocation (YYYY-MM-DD)

bookingMethodId.description: Booking method used for calculating the allocation

time.description: Time in minutes per day. Used when booking_method is per_day. E.g. 480 = 8 hours.

percentage.description: Percentage of daily capacity (0-100). Used when booking_method is percentage.

totalTime.description: Total time in minutes distributed across the full date range. Used when booking_method is total_hours.

maxCostPerHour.description: Maximum acceptable cost per hour for the requested resource

currency.description: Currency code for the cost rate (e.g. USD, EUR)

rejectedReason.description: Reason for rejection. Set when status is rejected.

createdAt.description: ISO timestamp when the resource request was created

resolvedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the request was resolved

canceledAt.description: ISO timestamp when the request was canceled. Only present when status is canceled.

timeToClose.description: Time to close the request in seconds (from creation to resolution/rejection/cancellation)

exchangeRate.description: Exchange rate applied to the cost

exchangeDate.description: Date the exchange rate was calculated

fields.description: System-defined person profile attributes as a key-value hash. Known keys: team_ids (array of team ID strings), job_title (string), subsidiary_id (workplace/subsidiary string ID), service_type_ids (array of service type ID strings), tags (array of tag name strings).

customFields.description: Employee custom field values. Key = custom field ID (string), value = serialized field value. Query custom fields to find available field IDs.

service.description: Service (budget line item) the resource is being requested for

creator.description: Person who created this resource request

resolver.description: Person who resolved or rejected this resource request

customFieldPeople.description: People linked via custom field values (no nested includes supported)

customFieldAttachments.description: Attachments linked via custom field values

roles.description: Permission set (also called a role) defines a named group of permissions that controls what a person can do in the organization. Roles are assigned to users to grant or restrict access to features such as project management, invoicing, time tracking, and administration. Each role targets a specific user type (employee, contractor, or client). Resolved permissions show the final effective permissions after inheritance is applied.
roles.descriptionShort: permission sets controlling what a person can do in the organization

salaries.description:
Salary (Cost Rate) defines a person's compensation and working capacity for a given period.
A person must have a cost rate to track time or be booked in the Resource Planner.

Each salary record defines:
- Compensation: a cost amount with a salary_type (hourly, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or annually) and currency.
- Hourly rate: auto-calculated from cost, working_hours, and salary_type. Used for cost calculations: Cost = worked hours × hourly rate.
- Working hours: array of hours per weekday (Mon–Sun). Defines the person's weekly capacity for availability calculations.
- Alternating hours: optional bi-weekly schedule (two different week patterns) for 9-day fortnights or 40/32-hour alternating weeks.
- Holiday calendar: assigned per salary period, determines which days are non-working for this person.
- Overhead: whether the person's time generates overhead cost for the organization (typically true for employees, false for contractors).

A person can have multiple salary records over time (e.g. raises, schedule changes). The active one is determined by date range (started_on/ended_on).
Hourly cost for fixed salary types is calculated using full-year capacity, not partial periods, to avoid inflated rates.
salaries.descriptionShort: people cost rates and working capacities with hourly cost, availability and overhead

defaultSalary.description: Legacy flag, always set to false for new records. Active cost rate is determined by date range (started_on/ended_on), not by this flag.

overhead.description: Does the salary generates overhead (cost) for the organization?

workingHours.description: Working hours for each day of the week (0-24). Can be for one week, or for two weeks if the "alternating hours" is enabled

alternatingHours.description: Defines whether working hours capacity is alternating between two weeks

cost.description: Gross compensation amount per pay period (depends on salary_type)

hourlyRate.description: Hourly rate of the salary (auto-calculated from cost, working_hours and salary_type)

holidayCalendar.description: Holiday calendar assigned to this salary period, affects availability calculations

scenario_bookings.description:
ScenarioBooking represents planned time allocation for a person assigned to a scenario service (ScenarioItem).
It is the resource-planning counterpart of a regular Booking, but scoped to a Scenario instead of a live Budget.

Use scenario bookings to model how many hours each person will work on each scenario service within a given date range.
This enables accurate cost and revenue forecasting in what-if scenarios before committing changes to a deal.

Time allocation methods (booking_method):
- Hours per day (1): fixed hours each working day of the booking period. Set the "time" field (minutes per day).
- Percentage (2): percentage of the person's daily capacity. Set the "percentage" field (0–100).
- Total hours (3): total hours spread evenly across the date range. Set the "total_time" field (minutes total).

Each booking must be created and updated individually — no bulk endpoints are available.

When the parent scenario is applied to the deal/budget, scenario bookings are converted into regular Resource Planner bookings.
scenario_bookings.descriptionShort: planned time allocation for a person on a scenario service — the resource-planning layer of a what-if scenario

time.description: Minutes per day. Required when booking_method is "1" (per day). Not used with other methods.

percentage.description: Percentage of daily capacity (0–100). Required when booking_method is "2" (percentage). Not used with other methods.

totalTime.description: Total minutes across the full date range. Required and writable when booking_method is "3" (total hours). Read-only for other methods — the API calculates it automatically.

totalWorkingDays.description: Number of working days in the booking date range. Read-only, calculated by the API.

bookingMethod.description: How time is allocated: per day ("1"), percentage ("2"), or total hours ("3").

note.description: Optional note or comment on the booking.

scenarioItem.description: The scenario item (person assigned to a scenario service) that this booking belongs to.

person.description: The person this booking is for (derived from the scenario item). person should never be passed on create or update — scenario_item should be passed instead.

scenario_items.description:
ScenarioItem assigns a person to a specific ScenarioService within a Scenario.
Each item represents one person's contribution to a service, with an optional date range
and rate overrides (price, cost, discount, markup).

Use scenario items to model who does the work and what it costs in a what-if scenario.
Supports bulk create, update, and delete for efficient multi-person assignment.

If person_id is set, the item name is derived from the person and cannot be set manually.
Rates default to the service's rates but can be overridden per item.
scenario_items.descriptionShort: people assignments on a scenario service with date range and rate

name.description: Name of the scenario item. Automatically set from the person when person is provided — do not set manually in that case.

startDate.description: Start date of this person's assignment on the service

endDate.description: End date of this person's assignment on the service

usedQuantity.description: Quantity of units (hours/days/pieces) assigned to this person on the service

discount.description: Discount percentage applied to this person's rate (0–100)

markup.description: Markup percentage applied to this person's rate

price.description: Billable rate override for this person in cents. Defaults to the scenario service rate.

cost.description: Cost rate override for this person in cents. Auto-calculated from person's salary if not provided.

currency.description: Currency code inherited from the scenario service

scenarioService.description: The scenario service this item belongs to

person.description: The person assigned to this service in the scenario

scenario.description: The scenario this item belongs to (via scenario service)

scenario_sections.description: ScenarioSection is a grouping of ScenarioServices within a Scenario, mirroring how Sections group Services within a Deal. Each scenario service belongs to exactly one scenario section.
scenario_sections.descriptionShort: groups of scenario services, mirroring deal sections

scenario.description: The scenario this section belongs to

scenario_services.description: ScenarioService is a service line item within a Scenario, equivalent to a Service on a Deal. It defines the deliverable, pricing, and billing configuration for a what-if scenario. Scenario services are created when building out a scenario's service configuration.
scenario_services.descriptionShort: service lines in a scenarios with deliverable, pricing, and billing configuration

price.description: Per-unit rate in cents (e.g. hourly rate). Required for fixed billing type. budget_total is computed as price x quantity.

budgetCapEnabled.description: Whether budget cap is enabled for this service

scenario.description: The scenario this service belongs to

scenarioSection.description: The scenario section this service belongs to

deal.description: The deal this scenario service is associated with

scenarios.description: Scenario is a financial what-if plan tied to a deal. Scenarios model different service configurations so users can compare financials before committing changes to a deal's actual services. Typical workflow: create → inspect → compare financials → apply.
scenarios.descriptionShort: what-if plans of deals which model service configurations to compare financials before committing

refresh.name: refresh
refresh.description: Recalculate the change counts on a scenario — updates the added/removed/updated service counts based on the current state of the deal. Use after changes have been made to the deal's services outside of the scenario.

overwrite.name: overwrite
overwrite.description: Apply the scenario to the deal, overwriting all existing services with the scenario's services. DESTRUCTIVE ACTION — use with caution and always require explicit user confirmation.

sync.name: sync
sync.description: Apply the scenario to the deal, syncing changes while preserving any services that are not in the scenario. Use this when you want to update only the services that are in the scenario, without affecting any other existing services on the deal.

copy.name: copy
copy.description: Copy a scenario to create a new scenario with the same services and sections.

deal.description: The deal this scenario belongs to

currency.description: Currency code for this scenario (e.g. EUR, USD). Should match the deal currency. Required on create.

copyServices.description: When true on create, automatically copies all sections and services from the deal into the scenario. Write-only — pass true to pre-fill the scenario from the deal.

budget.description: Total budget amount for this scenario in cents

revenue.description: Total revenue for this scenario in cents

cost.description: Total cost for this scenario in cents

profit.description: Profit for this scenario in cents (revenue minus cost)

margin.description: Profit margin for this scenario as a percentage (0-100 scale)

updatedAt.description: When the scenario was last updated

search-quick-results.description: Search Quick Result represents an item returned from the global quick search. Results span multiple resource types: people, projects, deals, companies, tasks, docs, and budgets. Each result includes the matched item name, resource type, status, and type-specific metadata (e.g., company name for projects, workflow status for tasks). Used by the global search tool to find records across all resource types.
search-quick-results.descriptionShort: global quick search results across resource types

icon.description: Icon of the search result record

sections.description:
Section is an ordered grouping of Services within a Deal or Budget.
Sections organize services into logical categories (e.g. by phase, department, deliverable type, or "Expenses").
Each service belongs to exactly one section; every deal/budget has at least one section.

Sections are visible in the budget editor, time tracking (Client → Project → Budget → Section → Service hierarchy),
invoicing (line items can be grouped by section), and reports.
Sections can be duplicated along with all their services to speed up budget setup.
sections.descriptionShort: service groupings within a deal/budget (e.g. by phase or deliverable type)

service_assignments.description:
Service Assignment is a junction record linking a person to a specific service within a deal/budget.
Used when the budget's tracking type is set to "Restricted by person" — only assigned people can track time and log expenses against that service.

If no one is assigned to a service, the restriction does not apply and everyone on the budget can track against it.
If the budget tracking type is not "Restricted by person", service assignments have no effect on tracking permissions.
Each assignment connects exactly one person to one service. A person can be assigned to multiple services, and a service can have multiple people assigned.
service_assignments.descriptionShort: person-service links for time/expense tracking restrictions

service_type_assignments.description:
Service Type Assignment is a junction record linking a person to a service type, indicating which categories of work they perform (e.g. Design, Development).
Only one assignment per service type per person.

Used in two contexts:
- Tracking restriction: when a budget's tracking type is "Restricted by service type", only people assigned to the service's service type can track time and log expenses.
- Resource planning: filters people by their qualified service types when scheduling work in the Resource Planner.

Managed on the person's profile or in the service types settings.
service_type_assignments.descriptionShort: person - service type links for tracking and resource planning restrictions

service_types.description:
Service Type categorizes the kinds of work an organization delivers (e.g. Design, Development, Project Management, Consulting).
Every service in a budget must have a service type assigned — it is mandatory.

Service types serve multiple purposes:
- Budget organization: categorize services for consistent structure across deals/budgets.
- Pricing: rate cards define prices per service type, standardizing billing rates.
- Tracking restrictions: when a budget uses "Restricted by service type", only people assigned to the service's type can track time.
- Profitability analysis: reports can analyze revenue, cost, and margin per service type across all budgets.
- Accounting sync: service types can be mapped to item/account codes in connected accounting tools (Xero, QuickBooks, etc.).

Service types can be merged (reassigning all services, assignments, and prices to the winner) or archived.
Initial service types are auto-generated based on the organization's industry during signup.
Configured by admins.

merge.name: merge
merge.description: Merge two service types into one. All services, assignments, and prices from the loser are reassigned to the winner. The loser is permanently deleted.

query.description: Keyword or phrase for partial name matching / filtering of service types

service_types.queryHints: Users typically care about active service types, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

services.description:
Service is a line item on a Deal or Budget representing a type of work to be delivered (e.g. Design, Development, Consulting).
Services are the foundation of financial tracking — they connect time tracking, resource scheduling, invoicing, and profitability analysis.

Each service is configured with:
- A billing type: Fixed (agreed price regardless of hours), Time & Materials (billed per actual hours/expenses), or Non-billable (internal tracking only, no revenue).
- A tracking unit: Hours, Days (person-days), or Pieces (discrete items/expenses). The unit determines what generates or assumes revenue.
- Tracking toggles: time tracking, expense tracking, and booking (resource scheduling) can each be enabled or disabled independently.

Revenue recognition depends on billing type and unit:
- T&M by hour/day: approved time entries and future bookings generate revenue.
- T&M by piece: only approved expenses generate revenue.
- Fixed by hour/day: service holds all revenue upfront; time entries and bookings assume (transfer) revenue from the service.
- Fixed by piece: only expenses assume revenue from the service.
- Non-billable: never generates revenue, only costs.

Services belong to a Section within their deal/budget and are categorized by a Service Type (e.g. Design, Development) for cross-project reporting.
Invoicing is done from budget services — deal services cannot be invoiced directly until the deal is won and work is transferred to a budget.
Services can be moved between deals/budgets while preserving all linked time entries, expenses, and bookings.
services.descriptionShort: line items on a deal/budget with billing type, tracking unit, linking time tracking, scheduling, and invoicing

move.name: move
move.description: Move this service to a different deal or budget. Provide target_id (the deal/budget ID to move to).

position.description: Position of the service in the Section

billable.description: Is the service billable to the client?

limitationType.description: How time & expense tracking limits are enforced: no_limitation, estimate_limitation, quantity_limitation, or budget_fully_used

quantity.description: Quantity in pieces, hours or days that service is budgeting for (e.g. 10 pieces, 10 hours, 10 days)

timeTrackingEnabled.description: Is time tracking enabled for the service?

expenseTrackingEnabled.description: Is expense tracking enabled for the service?

bookingTrackingEnabled.description: Is booking tracking enabled for the service?

budgetCapEnabled.description: Is budget cap enabled for the service?

workedTime.description: Time people actually worked on the service (affects costs)

billableTime.description: Billable time that will be communicated to the client Company (affects revenue)

estimatedTime.description: Estimated time for work to be done (used for internal planning and tracking)

budgetedTime.description: Defines total time budget on the service which is agreed upon with the client Company

slack_channel.description: Slack Channel represents a Slack channel connected to the organization via Slack integration. Read-only — channels are synced from Slack. Channels can be public or private, and active or archived. Used to configure Slack notifications for projects and tasks.
slack_channel.descriptionShort: slack channels connected to the organization via integration

slack_messages.description: Slack Message represents a message in a Slack channel. Can be read from channel history or thread replies, and sent via the connected Slack bot integration.
slack_messages.descriptionShort: readable and sendable slack channel messages

blocks.description: Slack Block Kit layout blocks — structured rich-text elements (sections, images, actions, etc.) that compose the message body.

slack_users.description: Slack User represents an active workspace member in the connected Slack organization. Read-only — users are fetched from Slack. Bot accounts and deactivated users are automatically excluded.
slack_users.descriptionShort: active workspace members in the connected Slack organization

name.description: Slack username (handle) without the @ prefix. Filters across name, display name, real name, and email.

statusText.description: Custom status text set by the user (e.g. "In a meeting").

statusEmoji.description: Custom status emoji set by the user (e.g. :calendar:).

subsidiaries.description:
Subsidiary represents a legal entity within an Organization (e.g. regional offices, divisions, brands).
Every organization has at least one subsidiary. Multiple subsidiaries are available on the Ultimate plan.

Subsidiaries hold the "Bill From" identity used on financial documents:
- Company name, tax ID, and address — appear on invoices, proposals, budgets, and purchase orders.
- Default bank account — populates the "Pay To" section on invoices and is used for e-invoicing.
- Default tax rate — pre-fills tax on invoices, deals, budgets, and expenses unless a client-specific rate is set.
- Logo — appears on exported PDFs.

Deals, budgets, invoices, purchase orders, expenses, and people (via cost rates) are each assigned to a subsidiary.
This enables per-entity financial reporting, multi-currency support, and correct tax handling across jurisdictions.
subsidiaries.descriptionShort: legal entities within an organization with billing identity, tax rates, and bank details

survey_field_options.description: Survey Field Option is a selectable choice for a dropdown (select) or multi-select survey field. Each option belongs to a specific survey field and has a display name, position for ordering, and optional color for visual distinction in the form.
survey_field_options.descriptionShort: selectable choices for a dropdown or multi-select survey field

survey_fields.description:
Survey Field defines a question or input within a form (survey).
Each field has a data type (text, number, date, select, multiselect, checkbox, file, etc.), can be marked as required or optional, and belongs to a specific survey.

Fields can optionally be linked to an origin custom field (origin_field) to reuse an existing field definition.
When linked, form submissions can prefill the corresponding custom field on tasks created via automation.

Select and multiselect fields have associated survey field options that define the available choices.
The global flag indicates whether the field is shared across forms or specific to one.
The customizable_type specifies which resource type the field maps to (e.g., tasks, deals, projects).
survey_fields.descriptionShort: form field — text, number, date, dropdown, checkbox, file

surveys.description:
Survey (Form) is a customizable form for collecting structured data within a project.
Forms are used for intake requests, client feedback, internal approvals, support tickets, and standardized data gathering.

Each form belongs to a project and contains configurable fields (text, number, date, dropdown, multi-select, checkbox, file upload, etc.).
Fields can be linked to existing custom fields to reuse definitions and prefill task custom fields on submission.

Forms can be shared with different access levels: project members only, everyone in the organization, or anyone with a public link.

Form submissions can trigger automations — creating tasks, sending Slack messages, or emails based on responses and conditions.
Responses are exportable to PDF, CSV, or XLS.
Forms can be duplicated within the same project or across projects.
surveys.descriptionShort: project forms for structured data collection — supports automations on submission

task_dependencies.description:
Task Dependency defines a directional relationship between two tasks.
Three dependency types:
- Blocking (type_id=1): the source task prevents the dependent task from starting. Visible in Gantt as an arrow.
- Waiting on (type_id=2): the source task cannot start until the dependent task completes. Visible in Gantt as an arrow.
- Linked (type_id=3): tasks are related but not blocking each other. Not shown in Gantt.

When a blocking/waiting task is closed, the dependency status updates on the dependent task.
Dependencies are lost when tasks are moved to another project.
Subtasks can also have dependencies. There is no limit on the number of dependencies per task.
task_dependencies.descriptionShort: task dependency relationships — blocking, waiting on, or linked

dependentTask.description: Target task that depends on the source task

task_lists.description:
Task List is an ordered collection of tasks within a Folder in a Project.
Task Lists structure work into groups without imposing a predefined meaning — users define what lists represent in their context (e.g. sprints, phases, categories, priorities).
Each task belongs to exactly one task list; each task list belongs to exactly one folder.

Task lists can be archived when no longer active. Archiving a task list does not change the status of tasks inside it.
When moving tasks between projects with different workflows, task statuses need to be mapped to the target project's workflow.
Task lists can be duplicated (with their tasks) within the same project or to another project.
task_lists.descriptionShort: task groupings within a folder (e.g. sprints, phases)

placement.description: Visual placement of the task list within the folder layout

task_lists.queryHints: Users typically care about active task lists, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for task lists should be: { status: {eq: '1'} }

tasks.description:
Task represents a unit of work that can be assigned to a person. Tasks live in Task Lists within Folders in a Project.

Task hierarchy:
- Tasks: standalone work items with an assignee, workflow status, dates, estimates, and tags.
- Subtasks: nested under a parent task (via parent_task_id). Cannot be moved independently — moving a parent moves its subtasks. Can be converted to standalone tasks and vice versa.
- Milestones: key deliverables or checkpoints (type_id distinguishes tasks from milestones).

Tasks progress through workflow statuses (e.g. To Do, In Progress, Done) defined by the project's Workflow. Statuses belong to three categories: not started, started, and closed.
Closing a parent task does not close its subtasks, and closing all subtasks does not close the parent.

Tasks can include: todo checklists, time estimates (initial_estimate, remaining_time), start/due dates, attachments, subscribers, and an activity feed with comments.
Time entries can be linked to tasks for granular tracking of what work was done against which budget service.
Bookings can be created directly from tasks when scheduling is enabled, linking resource planning to task-level work.
Tasks can be private (visible only to subscribers) or public (visible to all project members).
Dependencies between tasks define blocking, waiting, and linked relationships.
tasks.descriptionShort: assignable work items in a project with statuses, todos, subtasks and dependencies

typeId.description: Indicates if the task is a task or a milestone

taskType.description: Indicates if the task is a parent task or a subtask

private.description: Private tasks are only visible to task subscribers. Default is false.

dueTime.description: Specific time of day for the due date (HH:MM format)

remainingTime.description: Remaining time to be worked on the task (still an estimate)

workedTime.description: Time people worked on the task

assignee.description: Person assigned to the task - a responsible person to deliver the task

parentTask.description: Parent task (if the task is a subtask)

workflowStatusCategoryId.description: Filter by workflow status category: not started, started, or closed

taskListStatus.description: Filter tasks by their task list status (active or archived)

boardStatus.description: Filter tasks by their board (folder) status (active or archived)

subscriberIds.description: IDs of people subscribed to task updates. Project watchers should be subscribed on new tasks.

lastActivityAt.description: Date of last activity on the task

blockingDependencyCount.description: Number of tasks that this task is blocking

waitingOnDependencyCount.description: Number of tasks that this task is waiting on

tasks.queryHints:
Users typically care about open tasks in active projects, unless they specify otherwise. The default filter scope for tasks should be:
{ project.status: {eq: '1'}, task_list_status: {eq: '1'}, board_status: {eq: '1'}, workflow_status_category: {not_eq: '3'} }

tax_rates.description:
Tax Rate defines a tax or VAT percentage applied to invoices and other financial documents.
Each tax rate belongs to exactly one subsidiary — only tax rates matching the issuing subsidiary are available on a document.

Tax rates can have up to two components (e.g. federal + state tax), each with a name and percentage value.
An "empty" tax rate (no components) can be created for tax-exempt line items to avoid displaying "0% tax".

Tax rates are applied per line item on invoices, with a default pre-filled from the subsidiary or client company.
Once a tax rate has been used on a finalized invoice, it cannot be edited — duplicate it and create a new one instead.
Tax rates can be archived when outdated; archived rates remain on existing documents but cannot be applied to new ones.
tax_rates.descriptionShort: tax/vat percentages for subsidiaries — applied per line item on invoices

team_memberships.description:
Team Membership is a junction record linking a person to a team.
Create a membership to add a person to a team, delete it to remove them. A person can belong to multiple teams.
Team memberships enable team-based filtering, reporting, resource planning, and access sharing across projects, bookings, and time entries.

teams.description:
Team is a group of people who share skills, responsibilities, or work together on related projects (e.g., "Design", "Engineering", "People Ops").
A person can belong to multiple teams via team memberships.

Teams enable:
- Filtering and grouping across projects, bookings, time entries, and reports by team.
- Access sharing — team members can be granted shared access to projects and docs.
- Resource planning — view and manage team capacity in the Resource Planner.
- Reporting — team-based breakdowns of utilization, profitability, and workload.
teams.descriptionShort: groups of people, used for reporting and access sharing

time_entries.description:
Time Entry records work done by a person against a service in a deal or budget.
Time entries are the primary source of labor cost and revenue data in Productive.

Financial impact:
- Cost: worked time (time field) × person's hourly cost rate from their salary record.
- Revenue: billable time (billable_time) × service price. Only applies if the service is billable and the entry is approved.
- Billable time defaults to worked time but can differ (e.g. internal overhead logged against a billable service).

Tracking methods: manual entry, timer (start/stop), or automatic (from bookings, created at midnight for scheduled work).
Entries can optionally be linked to a Task for granular tracking of what work was done.

Lifecycle:
- Submission: if timesheet submission is enabled, entries must be submitted before they enter the approval queue.
- Approval: if time approval is enabled on the budget/deal, entries go through approved/rejected workflow. Only approved entries are recognized — they count toward budget usage, revenue, and can be invoiced.
- Invoicing: once an entry is included on a finalized invoice, it becomes locked and cannot be edited or moved.
- Financial lock: entries in a locked financial period cannot be modified.

A person must have a cost rate (salary) defined to track time.
time_entries.descriptionShort: logged work against services — drives cost, revenue, and profitability

billableTime.description: Billable time for the client. Defaults to the same value as time if not provided.

cost.description: Cost of the time entry. Calculated automatically from hours and person salary rate.

currency.description: Currency for the time entry. Inherited from the deal/organization.

approved.description: Is the time entry approved by the responsible person? System-managed through approval workflow.

approvedAt.description: Date when the time entry was approved

rejected.description: Is the time entry rejected by the responsible person? System-managed through approval workflow.

rejectedAt.description: Date when the time entry was rejected

rejectedReason.description: Reason for rejecting the time entry

submitted.description: Whether the time entry was submitted as a Timesheet. System-managed through submission workflow.

startedAt.description: When the work started, as an ISO 8601 datetime with timezone offset (e.g. "2026-03-12T09:00:00+01:00"). Sets the start position of the time entry on the timeline.

timerStartedAt.description: Time when the timer was started on this time entry

timerStoppedAt.description: Time when the timer was stopped on this time entry

trackMethodId.description: How the time was tracked. Auto-detected by the system.

person.description: Person who worked on the service

creator.description: Person who created the time entry. Defaults to the current user.

approver.description: Person who approved the time entry. Set by the system when approved.

rejecter.description: Person who rejected the time entry. Set by the system when rejected.

calendarEventId.description: ID of the calendar event this time entry was created from. Set to the string ID from the calendar event response.

timers.description:
Timer tracks an active time tracking session for a person.
Each timer is linked to a time entry and records elapsed time in real time.

When stopped, the tracked duration is saved to the associated time entry's worked time.
A person can only have one active timer at a time — starting a new timer automatically stops the previous one.
If a budget has overrun limitations and the timer reaches the service limit mid-session, it is automatically shortened.
Timers are available in the web app and desktop widget.
timers.descriptionShort: stopwatch session linked to a time entry — saved when stopped

personId.description: ID of the person who owns this timer

timeEntry.description: Time entry this timer is tracking for

timesheets.description:
Timesheet groups all time entries for a specific person on a specific date.
Acts as a daily aggregate container — one timesheet per person per day, containing all entries across different services and budgets.

Timesheets are the unit of submission in the approval workflow:
- Employees submit timesheets (by day or full week) to confirm their logged hours are ready for review.
- Once submitted, no new entries can be added for that day unless the timesheet is un-submitted.
- Managers can submit or un-submit timesheets on behalf of employees.
- A weekly timesheet is marked "Fully Submitted" only when all days (including weekends) are submitted.

After submission, individual time entries within the timesheet go through the approval flow (approved/rejected).
Rejected entries can be edited and are automatically resubmitted.
timesheets.descriptionShort: daily aggregates of time entries, unit of submission in approval workflow

person.description: Person who the timesheet is for

todos.description:
Todo is a checklist item within a Task or Deal. Todos break down work into smaller, trackable action items.
Each todo has a description, a position (order in the list), and can be assigned to a person and marked as closed (done).

Todos belong to exactly one parent — either a task (task_id) or a deal (deal_id), never both.
On tasks, todos serve as a lightweight checklist for sub-steps that don't warrant full subtasks.
On deals, todos help define next steps and sales actions to move opportunities through the pipeline.
todos.descriptionShort: checklist items on tasks or deals — assignable and completable

position.description: Position in the todo list of the task or deal

closed.description: Defines if the todo item is done

task.description: Task that this todo belongs to. Exactly one of task or deal must be provided.

deal.description: Deal that this todo belongs to. Exactly one of task or deal must be provided.

assignee.description: Person assigned to the todo item

closedAt.description: Timestamp of when the todo was completed. Null if the todo is still open.

todoableType.description: Type of the parent item — either tasks or deals.

dueDate.description: Due date for the todo. Only applicable when the todo belongs to a deal.

dueTime.description: Due time for the todo in HH:MM format. Only applicable when the todo belongs to a deal. Requires due_date to be set.

users.description: User represents a login account in Productive. Each user has an email for authentication and can be a member of multiple organizations. The user record is separate from the person record — a person is the organizational profile, while the user is the authentication identity. Users have feature flags that control access to experimental features.
users.defaultSort: Sorted by first_name, last_name (ascending) by default.

workflow_statuses.description:
Workflow Status is a named stage in a task's lifecycle within a workflow (e.g., "To Do", "In Review", "Done").
Each status belongs to one of three fixed categories that determine task state:
- Not Started (category_id=1): task is pending, work has not begun.
- Started (category_id=2): task is in progress.
- Closed (category_id=3): task is complete or resolved.

Multiple custom statuses can exist within each category, allowing fine-grained progress tracking.
The position field controls the display order of statuses within the workflow.
Tasks can be filtered and grouped by workflow status. Drag-and-drop between statuses is supported within a project.
workflow_statuses.descriptionShort: task statuses in a workflow in Not Started, Started, or Closed category

position.description: Position of the workflow status in the workflow

workflows.description:
Workflow defines the set of task statuses available in a project.
Each project is assigned one workflow, and different projects can use different workflows (e.g., a development workflow vs. a design workflow).

A workflow contains statuses organized into three fixed categories:
- Not Started: tasks that are pending, work has not begun.
- Started: tasks that are in progress.
- Closed: tasks that are complete or resolved.

Multiple custom statuses can be created within each category.
When switching a project's workflow, existing task statuses must be mapped to the new workflow's statuses.
Moving a status between categories (e.g., Started → Closed) immediately affects all tasks in that status across all projects using the workflow.
Workflows can be archived when no longer needed.
workflows.descriptionShort: task statuses groups, used in a project
